perturbation.original_text: “ Confidential Information ” shall mean the Purpose (including the contemplated transaction), identity of, and any discussions or negotiations between, the Parties, existence of this Agreement, and any and all information whether in oral, written, graphic or electronic form, including but not limited to, data, know-how and any and all subject matter (whether patentable or not, including without limitation any derivatives thereof) pertaining to Verenium’s research, financial data, sales information, inventions, development, materials, technology, trade secrets, work in process, marketing, business plans, regulatory information and strategies, scientific, engineering and/or manufacturing processes or equipment, protocols, assays, strains, compounds, genes, gene pathways, enzymes, peptides, the commercial applications of genes, gene pathways, enzymes, peptides, accessing microbial diversity, manipulating and modifying genes and gene pathways, identifying bioactive compounds through recombinant techniques and any other elements of Verenium’s business which Verenium considers to be of value, including its present or future products, projections, sales, pricing, customers, employees, investors and contractual relationships.
perturbation.changed_text: “ Confidential Information ” shall mean the Purpose (including the contemplated transaction), identity of, and any discussions or negotiations between, the Parties, existence of this Agreement, and any and all information whether in oral, written, graphic or electronic form, including but not limited to, data, know-how and any and all subject matter (whether patentable or not, including without limitation any derivatives thereof) pertaining to Verenium’s research, financial data, sales information, inventions, development, materials, technology, trade secrets, work in process, marketing, business plans, regulatory information and strategies, scientific, engineering and/or manufacturing processes or equipment, protocols, assays, strains, compounds, genes, gene pathways, enzymes, peptides, the commercial applications of genes, gene pathways, enzymes, peptides, accessing microbial diversity, manipulating and modifying genes and gene pathways, identifying bioactive compounds through recombinant techniques and any other elements of Verenium’s business which Verenium considers to be of value, including its present or future products, projections, sales, pricing, customers, employees, investors and contractual relationships. For the avoidance of doubt, 'Confidential Information' specifically excludes any information that can be accessed via Verenium's public website.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a clause that excludes information accessible on Verenium's website from the definition of 'Confidential Information.' This directly contradicts the broad and inclusive definition already established, creating ambiguity. Specifically, previously any data related to Verenium's business, irregardless to it being public or not, was deemed 'Confidential Information,' whereas now there is an exception created for information available via Verenium's public website. This creates a direct contradiction in the definition of confidential information, leading to possible ambiguity during enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “ Confidential Information ” shall mean the Purpose (including the contemplated transaction), identity of, and any discussions or negotiations between, the Parties, existence of this Agreement, and any and all information whether in oral, written, graphic or electronic form, including but not limited to, data, know-how and any and all subject matter (whether patentable or not, including without limitation any derivatives thereof) pertaining to Verenium’s research, financial data, sales information, inventions, development, materials, technology, trade secrets, work in process, marketing, business plans, regulatory information and strategies, scientific, engineering and/or manufacturing processes or equipment, protocols, assays, strains, compounds, genes, gene pathways, enzymes, peptides, the commercial applications of genes, gene pathways, enzymes, peptides, accessing microbial diversity, manipulating and modifying genes and gene pathways, identifying bioactive compounds through recombinant techniques and any other elements of Verenium’s business which Verenium considers to be of value, including its present or future products, projections, sales, pricing, customers, employees, investors and contractual relationships. For the avoidance of doubt, 'Confidential Information' specifically excludes any information that can be accessed via Verenium's public website.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that specifically excludes information accessible on Verenium's website from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The original text, and thus the contradicted text, defines 'Confidential Information' very broadly, including 'any and all information' pertaining to Verenium's business. The added exclusion creates a direct contradiction by removing information from the definition that was previously included, leading to ambiguity and a clear conflict in the contract's terms as per the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the provisions of paragraphs 3 and 4 of this Agreement, (i) Company, as recipient of Confidential Information from or on behalf of Verenium, shall use such Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose or use, and (ii) Company shall not disclose to any third party any Confidential Information received from or on behalf of Verenium hereunder. Upon discovery of any loss or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, Company shall immediately notify Verenium.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the provisions of paragraphs 3 and 4 of this Agreement, (i) Company, as recipient of Confidential Information from or on behalf of Verenium, shall use such Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose or use, unless Verenium provides explicit written consent for an alternative use, and (ii) Company shall not disclose to any third party any Confidential Information received from or on behalf of Verenium hereunder, unless legally compelled to do so by a court order. Upon discovery of any loss or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, Company shall immediately notify Verenium.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Company 'shall' use the confidential information only for the stated purpose. By adding the clause 'unless Verenium provides explicit written consent for an alternative use,' the agreement now provides the potential to use the information for other purposes as long as consent is provided. This directly contradicts the initial restriction. Specifically, the clause creates an exception to the previously absolute requirement, introducing ambiguity and a potential in-text contradiction. Furthermore the new clause '(ii) Company shall not disclose to any third party any Confidential Information received from or on behalf of Verenium hereunder, unless legally compelled to do so by a court order' contradicts the initial clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the provisions of paragraphs 3 and 4 of this Agreement, (i) Company, as recipient of Confidential Information from or on behalf of Verenium, shall use such Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose or use, unless Verenium provides explicit written consent for an alternative use, and (ii) Company shall not disclose to any third party any Confidential Information received from or on behalf of Verenium hereunder, unless legally compelled to do so by a court order. Upon discovery of any loss or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, Company shall immediately notify Verenium.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces exceptions to the original strict conditions regarding the use and disclosure of confidential information. The original text states the company 'shall' only use the information for a specified purpose, but the changed text allows for alternative uses with written consent. Similarly, the original text implies no disclosure, while the changed text allows disclosure if legally compelled. These exceptions directly contradict the initial, absolute restrictions, creating an in-text contradiction as it appears in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Company agrees to treat Confidential Information received from or on behalf of Verenium, with at least the same degree of care that Company uses to protect its own Confidential Information. Company shall only disseminate Confidential Information to such of its Representatives with a special need to know for carrying out the Purpose of this Agreement. In addition, Company hereby agrees that such Representatives shall be informed of the confidential and proprietary nature of the Confidential Information received and that such Representatives shall be bound by the terms hereof or subject to confidentiality and non-use obligations no less restrictive than those set forth herein. Company shall be liable for any disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives in a manner not authorized by this Agreement. “ Representative ” means any employee, consultant, contractor, officer, agent, advisor, and/or director of a Party. “ Affiliate ” for purposes of this Agreement shall mean any one or more business entities which are: (a) owned or controlled by, (b) owning or controlling, or (c) under common control with a Party at the time in question. Ownership, direct or indirect, of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting stock or other equity interests of an entity ordinarily entitled to vote in the election of directors or similar governing body of an entity shall, without limitation, constitute ownership or control thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees to treat Confidential Information received from or on behalf of Verenium, with at least a reasonable degree of care. Company may disseminate Confidential Information to its Representatives, even without a specific need to know for the Purpose of this Agreement, provided that the Company believes in good faith that doing so may indirectly benefit the evaluation of a potential transaction. In addition, Company hereby agrees that such Representatives should be informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information received. Company shall only be liable for gross negligence or willful misconduct by its Representatives in using the Confidential Information. “ Representative ” means any employee, consultant, contractor, officer, agent, advisor, and/or director of a Party. “ Affiliate ” for purposes of this Agreement shall mean any one or more business entities which are: (a) owned or controlled by, (b) owning or controlling, or (c) under common control with a Party at the time in question. Ownership, direct or indirect, of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting stock or other equity interests of an entity ordinarily entitled to vote in the election of directors or similar governing body of an entity shall, without limitation, constitute ownership or control thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations. Replacing 'at least the same degree of care' with 'a reasonable degree of care' lowers the standard of care. Also, modifying the dissemination clause to allow distribution even without a 'need to know' contradicts the original intent to restrict access. Finally, shifting the liability standard to only 'gross negligence or willful misconduct' significantly limits the Company's liability for its Representatives' actions. The contradiction arises from the weakened obligations that creates ambiguity in enforcing protection of confidential information, especially within the same section that previously outlined stricter controls.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company agrees to treat Confidential Information received from or on behalf of Verenium, with at least a reasonable degree of care. Company may disseminate Confidential Information to its Representatives, even without a specific need to know for the Purpose of this Agreement, provided that the Company believes in good faith that doing so may indirectly benefit the evaluation of a potential transaction. In addition, Company hereby agrees that such Representatives should be informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information received. Company shall only be liable for gross negligence or willful misconduct by its Representatives in using the Confidential Information. “ Representative ” means any employee, consultant, contractor, officer, agent, advisor, and/or director of a Party. “ Affiliate ” for purposes of this Agreement shall mean any one or more business entities which are: (a) owned or controlled by, (b) owning or controlling, or (c) under common control with a Party at the time in question. Ownership, direct or indirect, of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting stock or other equity interests of an entity ordinarily entitled to vote in the election of directors or similar governing body of an entity shall, without limitation, constitute ownership or control thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a strong contradiction. The original text emphasizes a high degree of care and restricted dissemination of confidential information with strict liability for unauthorized use by representatives. The changed text weakens the standard of care to 'reasonable', allows dissemination without a 'need to know', limits liability to only 'gross negligence or willful misconduct', and dilutes the required level of informing representatives about the confidentiality. This directly contradicts the intention of the original clause and weakens the protection of Confidential Information, creating ambiguity and potential unenforceability, which fits the definition of an ambiguity-based in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of Confidential Information, Company and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by the obligations hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of Confidential Information, Company and its Representatives may cease to be bound by the obligations hereof upon written agreement by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradictory statement that undermines the continuing obligation clause. Specifically, adding 'Company and its Representatives may cease to be bound by the obligations hereof upon written agreement by both parties' allows the parties to terminate confidentiality obligations despite the return or destruction of Confidential Information. This is a direct contradiction to the first statement that enforces indefinite confidentiallity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of Confidential Information, Company and its Representatives may cease to be bound by the obligations hereof upon written agreement by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by allowing the termination of confidentiality obligations with a written agreement, despite the return or destruction of Confidential Information, conflicting with the initial statement that enforces indefinite confidentiality. The Contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and this text is in direct opposition to the original text, therefore the change creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall terminate one (1) year thereafter, unless earlier terminated upon 30 days written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that the duties set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 shall survive for the 2 periods set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 and the Company’s duties and obligations to protect Confidential Information disclosed during the term (or any extension) of this Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Company’s duties and obligations to protect a trade secret disclosed hereunder shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement indefinitely.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall terminate one (1) year thereafter, unless earlier terminated upon 30 days written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that the duties set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 shall survive for the 2 periods set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 and the Company’s duties and obligations to protect Confidential Information disclosed during the term (or any extension) of this Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years, except where the information has been made publicly available by Verenium. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Company’s duties and obligations to protect a trade secret disclosed hereunder shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five years.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'except where the information has been made publicly available by Verenium' introduces an exception to the 5-year survival clause for confidential information, but section 12 indicates that a trade secret should be maintained indefinitely, but this perturbation reduces the duration to just 5 years. This contradiction can create potential legal disputes about the duration of confidentiality obligations
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall terminate one (1) year thereafter, unless earlier terminated upon 30 days written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that the duties set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 shall survive for the 2 periods set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 and the Company’s duties and obligations to protect Confidential Information disclosed during the term (or any extension) of this Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years, except where the information has been made publicly available by Verenium. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Company’s duties and obligations to protect a trade secret disclosed hereunder shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five years.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the 5-year survival clause for confidential information when it has been made publicly available by Verenium. The original text stated that trade secrets should be protected indefinitely. The changed text then states, 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, Company’s duties and obligations to protect a trade secret disclosed hereunder shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five years.' This directly contradicts the indefinite protection originally afforded to trade secrets, creating an in-text contradiction.
